content.text:
<user_query>
Task class: Contractable
Objective snapshot: Make PlotJuggler launches via `~/.cursor/scripts/play_motion_rl.sh` deterministic and safe, while ensuring `humanoid-gym/humanoid/scripts/play_interactive.py` emits explicit HUD initialization status and handles headless/GUI probe failures cleanly in interactive mode.
Pre-conditions: plotjuggler runs on ${JUMP_HOST} with display configured.
Post-conditions: (1) launch uses `--layout` path with explicit validated quoting and default fallback unchanged; (2) cleanup terminates only the spawned PlotJuggler session using captured remote/local PID + narrow checks; (3) interactive mode logs HUD attempt, success/failure reason, and a deterministic outcome (continue without HUD in non-fatal mode or fail fast if explicit env flag requires); (4) diagnostic messages distinguish layout load issues from runtime start failures.
Invariants: keep TASK, LAYOUT, INTERACTIVE defaults and existing argument parsing/non-interactive flow unchanged; avoid broad rewrites and no plan file edits.
Non-conditions: no PlotJuggler/IsaacGym stack rewrite.
Proposed implementation details: capture PlotJuggler PID from launcher invocation, add a cleanup trap that uses that PID and, if possible, host-local process owner filtering; enforce quoted remote command with explicit arg expansion; add explicit `--layout` existence check + print chosen path + failure mode before launch; in `play_interactive.py`, wrap cv2 window setup in try/except that logs backend/GUI probe errors and either logs warning + disables HUD in interactive path or exits with clear error per explicit `--interactive` contract.
</user_query>

task_class: Contractable
objective_coverage: incomplete
contract_validity:
  - issue: Contract references an explicit fail-fast HUD mode but does not define the exact predicate/flag contract (name, default, precedence over `--interactive`), making post-condition #3 not fully testable.
    severity: high
  - issue: Post-condition #1 (“default fallback unchanged”) is underspecified against existing behavior; it is not explicit whether invalid `LAYOUT` should force a hard failure or revert to default automatically.
    severity: medium
  - issue: Remote PlotJuggler execution contract in post-condition #2 assumes reliable PID ownership semantics across SSH/local boundaries but does not state how to capture and verify that process ownership is guaranteed on both local and remote hosts.
    severity: medium

proposed_change_issues:
  - issue: PID capture + cleanup strategy in SSH context is the main risk area; if `play_motion_rl.sh` still relies on `pgrep`/name matching instead of a single captured session PID, it can violate “terminate only spawned session.”
    severity: high
    post_condition_ref: (2)
  - issue: The proposed HUD handling may still leave a non-deterministic branch unless both success/failure reasons and selected fallback behavior are keyed off a single explicit flag (non-fatal vs fatal) and consistently logged.
    severity: high
    post_condition_ref: (3)
  - issue: Distinguishing “layout load issues” from runtime start failures requires separate detection points (pre-flight layout validation + post-launch runtime failure parse); combining all stderr into one generic error can fail post-condition #4.
    severity: medium
    post_condition_ref: (4)

general_findings:
  - issue: Existing non-interactive argument parsing is a strict invariant; adding launch-time validation and HUD probing in shared paths could accidentally alter behavior if not isolated to interactive flow.
    severity: medium
  - issue: Current requirement avoids broad changes, so touching script quoting/ssh command structure should be minimal to avoid regressions in existing launch paths.
    severity: medium

outliers_gotchas:
  - risk: On systems without display (or with headless OpenCV backend), `cv2` GUI probe may raise early exceptions before a useful HUD status message is emitted unless exceptions are caught around all HUD touchpoints.
    mitigation: Wrap entire HUD init block (backend probe, window create, write ops) in one deterministic try/except and emit explicit reason tags.
  - risk: Remote PlotJuggler process may be detached from launcher (e.g., session managers, shell wrappers), so `$!` may not map to final GUI PID.
    mitigation: Use explicit shell-side PID capture (e.g., remote wrapper writes child PID to a temp file) and have trap read/kill that exact PID when available; fall back to guarded name+uid check only as last-resort.
  - risk: If explicit fail-fast HUD flag is user-configurable, ambiguous semantics between CLI and env can silently change behavior.
    mitigation: define one authoritative switch and document precedence (e.g., `--hud-required` > env var > default warn-and-continue).

decision: Iterate
reason: High-severity ambiguity around HUD fail-fast semantics and remote PID ownership means the proposed implementation can miss contractual post-conditions unless those behaviors are made explicit before editing.
